text: Prangos ferulacea, also known as Ferula or Ferula root, is a plant that is commonly used in Iran, in both cooking and as a traditional medicine. The present study investigated the phytochemical composition and possible biological effects of the Prangos ferulacea extract, obtained by maceration. The HPLC analysis of the methanolic extract of the P. ferulacea revealed the presence of some compounds among which p-coumaric acid was dominated. The FTIR analysis of the extract displayed several absorption bands attributed to phenolic compounds, flavonoids, tannins and carboxylic acids. The results revealed that the total phenolic content (TPC) and total flavonoid content (TFC) of the extract were 202.04 ± 5.46 mg GAE/g and 1909.46 ± 13 μg QE/g, respectively. Moreover, the amount of beta-carotene in P. ferulacea extract was 2.91 mg/100 g. The extract showed strong scavenging activity against DPPH (IC50: 274 ± 7.2 μg/mL) and ABTS (IC50: 120.45 ± 9.6 μg/mL) radicals, as well as ferric reducing antioxidant power (FRAP) of 1.92 ± 0.05 μg AAE/g. In addition, the extract exhibited significant cytotoxic effects on the HT-29 cancer cells and IC50 value was obtained as 82.15 ± 0.02 μg/mL. According to the results of the disk and well diffusion methods and MIC and MBC values, Listeria monocytogenes had the most sensitivity to P. ferulacea extract (MIC and MBC = 16 and 128 mg/mL, respectively). Also, SEM and CLSM images showed destructive changes in L. monocytogenes cells after treating with P. ferulacea extract. On the other hand, gene expression analysis revealed that P. ferulacea extract inhibited the expression of fbf and icaS genes which are responsible for biofilm formation.
